wave-splitting.description: Splits large bug lists into sequential waves, one executor per wave to reduce context rot. Default wave size 3 normal, 5 UAT

cross-wave-handoff.signature: Modified-files list and commit log passed from wave N to wave N+1
cross-wave-handoff.description: Context propagation between waves to avoid regressions

sequential-execution.description: Waves execute strictly sequentially on same branch; no parallel wave execution

wave-results-display.description: Each wave's results displayed before proceeding to next wave

backward-compatible.description: Without --wave-size flag, /rapid:bug-fix behaves exactly as before

definition.scope: Add multi-agent wave splitting to /rapid:bug-fix so large bug lists are split into sequential waves with context handoff.

tasks.description: Add wave decomposition logic splitting bugs into groups of --wave-size
Implement sequential wave dispatch with one executor per wave
tasks.acceptance: Each wave gets a fresh agent with only its assigned bugs

tasks.description: Implement cross-wave context handoff (modified-files + commit log)
tasks.acceptance: Wave 2+ agents receive prior wave changes

tasks.description: Add --wave-size flag with defaults (3 normal, 5 UAT)
Display per-wave results and aggregate summary
tasks.acceptance: User sees wave progress and final summary

acceptance: Context handoff prevents regressions across waves
Backward compatible when wave splitting not triggered
